summary: Collect adviser intervention evidence for Alpha flows
acceptance_criteria: Evidence records whether adviser interventions help players understand Alpha goals, risks, diplomacy, legitimacy, succession, policy/event choices, campaign consequences, and victory progress without taking control away from the player.
Evidence distinguishes useful intervention, ignored intervention, harmful intervention, and missing intervention using anonymized/local observation or deterministic run review only.
The task records blocker defects and bounded follow-ups without expanding product scope, adding telemetry, collecting personal data, or deciding M6 exit by itself.
Cultural or historical concerns found during adviser text review are tagged and routed to historical_researcher or Human Gate before formal content acceptance.
required_tests: Adviser evidence lists session/run count, local privacy handling, intervention classifications, blocker/follow-up split, and no telemetry/network collection.
notes: This is Alpha evidence only; it does not authorize telemetry or external research without a separate gate.
Do not modify product code in this evidence task.
If evidence needs new adviser behavior, route a separate allowed implementation task instead of expanding this one.
next_action: Start M6-ALPHA-NO-P0P1-VALIDATION-001 as the highest-priority dependency-ready M6 task unless taskctl ready changes after closure merge.

evidence_summary.method: Local privacy-safe design review of accepted M6 client/adviser surfaces plus deterministic batch-run evidence. No new external participant session was conducted and no telemetry or network collection was added.

session_and_run_count.batch_basis: M6-BATCH-AUTORUN-BALANCE-DASHBOARD-001 accepted artifact hash 99d64950, 3 scenarios x 2 seeds.
session_and_run_count.client_coverage_basis: Accepted M6 client QA records 10 Chromium Playwright tests covering Alpha flow, forbidden UI entry checks, and viewport overflow checks.

privacy_handling.notes: Evidence is based on local repository files, accepted handoffs, deterministic run summaries, and code/test read-through. No player identity, input transcript, screen recording, remote analytics, or authoritative save/world payload is stored by this task.

target.goal: Record whether the current Alpha adviser intervention layer helps understanding without taking player control.
target.non_goal: This evidence does not approve M6 exit, change product scope, add telemetry, tune AI, implement adviser behavior, or accept formal historical/cultural content.

player_decisions.decision: Use the adviser primary recommendation or choose another command path.
player_decisions.observed_advantage: The primary reason m6.adviser.recognized-order-ready ties the Alpha win path to recognized order instead of map coloring.
player_decisions.cost: Raw reason codes and scores add cognitive load and can look like debug output rather than player-facing advice.
player_decisions.dependency: Requires M6 client read models, terminal evidence, diplomacy/legitimacy/succession reason codes, and command preview/confirm surfaces.
player_decisions.counter: The player can ignore the candidate; rival refusal, succession timing, policy consequences, and campaign/postwar costs can still make a different command correct.

player_decisions.decision: Compare recognition, wait-for-succession, and policy-event candidates.
player_decisions.observed_advantage: Multiple candidates preserve agency and show that a no-command wait can be a legitimate option.
player_decisions.cost: The lower-scored wait candidate can be ignored because it lacks a clear trigger for when waiting is better.
player_decisions.dependency: Depends on visible candidate ids, decision kinds, command kind or no-command state, score, and reason chips.
player_decisions.counter: A future intervention should explain the tradeoff condition rather than raising a hidden global penalty for ignoring advice.

player_decisions.decision: Choose policy/event options while checking encyclopedia links.
player_decisions.observed_advantage: The policy/event panel ties options to consequences, encyclopedia references, and content tags.
player_decisions.cost: The adviser only surfaces a policy candidate and reason codes; it does not yet summarize consequence horizon or obligation risk in plain language.
player_decisions.dependency: Depends on policy/event runtime, encyclopedia read model, option reason codes, and M6 client surface coverage.
player_decisions.counter: Players can reject or delay policy choices; follow-up should add clearer consequence framing without making the adviser auto-select.

player_decisions.decision: Judge whether campaign/postwar consequences support victory progress.
player_decisions.observed_advantage: Terminal and batch evidence expose recognized-by count, legitimacy, succession, postwar arrangement, route, population, and policy-event evidence.
player_decisions.cost: The adviser panel itself does not yet give a direct campaign consequence intervention when supply, casualties, or postwar loss should change the plan.
player_decisions.dependency: Depends on M4 campaign/postwar results, M6 terminal evidence, batch aggregate metrics, and M6 client terminal status.
player_decisions.counter: The opponent can wait out supply or exploit postwar weakness; missing intervention is bounded as follow-up, not a hidden punishment.

reviewed_surfaces: M6 adviser read model: primaryReasonCode, commandKind, candidate ids, decisionKind, score, reasonCodes.
M6 terminal read model: victory/failure status, recognizedByCount, legitimacyScoreBps, checkpoint, victory requirements, failure conditions, excluded surfaces.
M6 diplomacy/legitimacy/succession panel: relations, recognition, legitimacy, succession continuity, agreement/source reason codes.
M6 policy/event/encyclopedia panel: active options, resolved event, modifiers, content tags, linked reason codes.
M6 batch artifact summary: 3 scenarios, 2 seeds, 6 runs, victory/continued-play/defeat/command-rejected outcomes.
state.control_boundary: Adviser state is read-model evidence. It displays candidates and reason codes, but player commands still go through preview/confirm and authoritative GameCommand submission.

update_frequency.ui_starting_assumption: Adviser interventions should update after a new Alpha read-model revision or player command step, not on real time or frame time.
update_frequency.metric: Intervention reason changes should be traceable to command/revision/day/terminal evidence, not hidden timers.
update_frequency.validation_way: Future live-worker integration can compare read-model revision changes with reason summary changes. Current evidence is fixture/read-model and deterministic-run review only.

candidate_scores.starting_hypothesis: Fixture scores 82, 63, and 41 are ordering evidence only, not final balance values.
candidate_scores.metric: A candidate is useful when its visible advantage, cost, dependency, and counterplay are understandable and it does not become an auto-play instruction.
candidate_scores.validation_way: Future consented observation can track whether players can explain why they accepted, ignored, or rejected an intervention. No telemetry is authorized.

invariants: No telemetry, analytics, network collection, accounts, paid services, or personal data collection.
No product code, package code, content-source data, tools, docs/gate files, manifests, checksums, or release artifacts are modified by this task.
Adviser intervention evidence cannot decide M6 exit; M6-ALPHA-NO-P0P1-VALIDATION-001 and M6-EXIT-VALIDATION-001 remain separate.
Adviser candidates must not auto-submit commands or bypass player confirmation.
AI and player actions must continue to use the same GameCommand payload class.
Historical/cultural content tags remain review markers, not formal production acceptance.

observed: Layered AI evidence records payload-level parity for non-system AI commands against player templates.
Adviser panel displays AI/adviser reasons and includes m6.adviser.no-hidden-rescue.
Deterministic batch evidence records no-rescue completion across local Alpha runs without remote collection.
ai_behavior.limit: The current adviser read model is still a projection over accepted protocol/read-model scripts and deterministic runs. It is not yet a measured live-player intervention loop.

helps: Shows AI/adviser primary reason, command kind, candidate list, decision kind, score, and reason chips.
Shows diplomacy, legitimacy, succession, policy/event, encyclopedia, map candidate, and terminal status in adjacent non-placeholder panels.
Shows victory/failure requirements and excluded manual node battle surface.
risks: Reason codes are precise but not yet fully player-facing prose.
Candidate scores can imply false precision if not paired with visible costs and dependencies.
Campaign/postwar consequence advice is visible in surrounding panels but not yet a direct adviser intervention.

classification_table.evidence: Client and QA handoffs show M6 adviser reasons plus terminal/victory panels; tests assert the reason is present in M6 Alpha UI.
classification_table.control_risk: Low in current evidence because it is displayed as a candidate and commands still require player preview/confirm.
classification_table.bounded_follow_up: Translate reason code to concise player-facing explanation before broader player sessions.

classification_table.evidence: Policy/event panel exposes Harbor charter petition options, consequences, encyclopedia references, and content tag COMPOSITE.
classification_table.control_risk: Low because the command kind is visible and the UI remains command-driven.
classification_table.bounded_follow_up: Add clearer consequence horizon and obligation/campaign linkage in a later allowed client/design task.

classification_table.evidence: Candidate is visible with no command and lower score, but this task has 0 new human sessions, so actual ignore rate is unknown.
classification_table.control_risk: No auto-control risk, but the advice may be ignored because it lacks a plain condition for waiting.
classification_table.bounded_follow_up: Future consented observation or deterministic scripted review should ask whether players know when waiting is better.

classification_table.intervention: Raw reason-code and numeric-score presentation if treated as final player-facing copy
classification_table.evidence: The UI shows exact reason codes and fixture scores. These are useful audit evidence but can push players toward formula hunting.
classification_table.control_risk: Medium as a comprehension risk, not an authority risk; it does not take control but can obscure the actual tradeoff.
classification_table.bounded_follow_up: Keep reason codes available for audit, but add short explanatory labels before external/non-author player sessions.

classification_table.evidence: M4/M6 surrounding panels and batch metrics expose route, supply, war/postwar loss, recognized order, and terminal evidence, but the adviser candidate list does not directly call out supply/casualty/postwar-risk tradeoffs.
classification_table.control_risk: No current auto-control risk. Missing guidance can make campaign consequences feel disconnected from adviser advice.
classification_table.bounded_follow_up: Add a future adviser candidate or explanation that cites supply, casualty, withdrawal, and postwar arrangement evidence without adding telemetry.

classification_table.intervention: Measured useful/ignored/harmful rates from real players
classification_table.evidence: This task used local deterministic review only. It did not run new consented participant sessions.
classification_table.control_risk: None in product behavior; evidence confidence is the limitation.
classification_table.bounded_follow_up: If stronger evidence is required, run separate consented anonymous local sessions under a new task, with no telemetry by default.

bounded_follow_ups.summary: Convert reason-code-heavy adviser output into concise player-facing labels while keeping audit codes available.
bounded_follow_ups.scope_guard: Future client/design task only; no telemetry, no auto-play, no content lock.

bounded_follow_ups.summary: Add or test a direct adviser intervention for supply, casualty, withdrawal, and postwar consequence tradeoffs.
bounded_follow_ups.scope_guard: Use existing M4/M6 read models and command previews; do not add manual node battle or hidden global penalties.

bounded_follow_ups.summary: Optional separate consented anonymous local sessions to measure accepted/ignored/harmful interventions if lead wants stronger player evidence.
bounded_follow_ups.scope_guard: Separate Human/lead-approved research task; no telemetry, remote analytics, accounts, or personal data.

bounded_follow_ups.summary: Route any future formal names, symbols, language, cultural framing, or production encyclopedia copy to historical_researcher before content acceptance.
bounded_follow_ups.scope_guard: Current content tags COMPOSITE/FICTIONAL are not formal historical acceptance.

cultural_historical_concerns.concern: Current policy/event and map/encyclopedia labels include COMPOSITE/FICTIONAL framing. They are acceptable as Alpha review markers but not formal historical content acceptance.
cultural_historical_concerns.human_gate: Required only if a later task tries to lock major cultural-risk content, symbols, formal names, or disputed interpretation.

boundaries: Evidence task only; no product implementation.
No hidden global punishment is proposed as an adviser consequence.
No formal historical or cultural content is accepted by this writer.

exploits.risk: Players treat adviser score as a solved optimal move.
exploits.counter: Show costs, dependencies, and counterplay; keep alternative candidates and no-command wait visible.
Keep audit codes but add plain labels in a future bounded task.
Maintain preview/confirm command path and never auto-submit adviser commands.

exploits.risk: Dashboard or observation evidence becomes telemetry by creep.
exploits.counter: Keep artifacts local, schema-versioned, and manually reviewed; no remote collection.

exploits.risk: Scenario or encyclopedia text locks unreviewed historical interpretation.
exploits.counter: Keep COMPOSITE/FICTIONAL tags and route formal content to historical_researcher or Human Gate.

vertical_slice_acceptance: Gameplay_designer emits REVIEW evidence only and does not approve its own output.
Evidence covers useful, ignored, harmful-risk, and missing intervention categories.
Evidence records 0 new human/external observation sessions and 6 reviewed deterministic runs.
Evidence preserves local privacy boundaries and excludes telemetry/network collection.
Evidence records no blocker defects and bounded follow-ups for plain-language reasons, campaign consequence advice, optional consented sessions, and historical routing.
Independent qa_reviewer must accept or request changes before lead integration.

accepted_tests_run.result: Only QA handoff was untracked before lead ACCEPT; no forbidden path changes.
Task list completed; M6-ADVISER-INTERVENTION-EVIDENCE-001 was REVIEW before lead ACCEPT.
No dependency-ready tasks printed while current task remained REVIEW.

non_blocking_risks.summary: Writer REVIEW handoff internal commit field points to 9a0d597, while task metadata, route evidence, and QA review target pin c1edb91f as the accepted review commit.
non_blocking_risks.disposition: Do not rewrite the already-reviewed writer handoff; lead ACCEPT records c1edb91f as authoritative.
